latest.title: Judgment Delivered in Commercial Litigation Claim
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 8002W
Litigation Capital Management Ltd
17 March 2026
17 March 2026
Litigation Capital Management Limited
("
LCM
" or the "
Company
")
Judgment Delivered in Commercial Litigation Claim
Litigation Capital Management Limited ("LCM" or the "Company"), an alternative asset manager specialising in dispute financing solutions internationally, announces that an Australian Court has delivered judgment in a commercial litigation claim funded by the Company.
On 11 March 2026, LCM announced that it anticipated a judgment on a small case investment. This judgment has now been delivered, with the Court finding against LCM's funded party.
LCM funded the case with A$1.4 million in shareholder invested capital. A policy of ATE insurance is in place to protect against adverse costs risk.
LCM is currently reviewing the judgment and assessing potential next steps alongside the funded party and legal representatives.
Enquiries
Litigation Capital Management
Patrick Moloney, Chief Executive Officer
David Collins, Chief Financial Officer
Cavendish (Nomad and Broker)
Tel: 020 7220 0500
Jonny Franklin-Adams and Isaac Hooper (Corporate Finance)
Ella Bedford (Corporate Broking)
Note: This announcement contains inside information which is disclosed in accordance with the Market Abuse Regulation.
www.lcmfinance.com

latest.title: Update on Case Investment and Strategic Review
latest.content:
Today 09:34
RNS Number : 2269W
Litigation Capital Management Ltd
11 March 2026
11 March 2026
Litigation Capital Management Limited
("
LCM
" or the "
Company
")
Update on Case Investment and Strategic Review
Litigation Capital Management Limited ("LCM" or the "Company"), an alternative asset manager specialising in dispute financing solutions internationally, is pleased to announce that its funded party has been successful in the High Court of Australia in a trademark dispute claim it funded on behalf of Katie Perry (Designer), a Sydney-based fashion designer against Katy Perry (Singer) and her related corporate entities.
The claim was initially brought in the Federal Court of Australia where there was a finding of infringement of the Designer's trademark by the Singer and one of her related corporate entities. The Singer and her related corporate entities appealed this decision to the Full Court of the Federal Court who found, among other things, that the Designer's trademark should be cancelled as she had applied for the trademark knowing of the Singer's reputation. The Designer applied for special leave to appeal to the High Court of Australia which was granted on 11 April 2025. Following a hearing before the High Court of Australia on 9 September 2025, by majority, the appeal was successful.
The matter will now go back to the Full Court in respect of outstanding issues, including costs of the proceeding before the primary judge and the Full Court. Following that, the damages need to be quantified and at that point we will be able to determine LCM's return on investment. LCM has invested A$3.3m of shareholder capital into this claim (it is a balance sheet investment without any participation of fund investors) and it has been held in LCM's financial statements at a value of A$3.3m.
The Company also anticipates a judgment on another small case investment, with A$1.4 million shareholder invested capital, within the next week. The outcome will be announced when the judgment has been handed down.
LCM will be finalising the date of its half-year results presentation in the coming weeks, and this will then be communicated to investors. The presentation is expected to be later than usual, around the end of March, in order to increase the likelihood of being able to give shareholders a meaningful update on the outcome of the Strategic Review.
The Company will make further announcements as and when appropriate.
Patrick Moloney, CEO of LCM, commented:
"LCM has provided funding for this claim from its balance sheet since 2019. It is a classic instance of disputes finance enabling a claimant to bring a claim which they would otherwise not have the resources to fund. The case was successful at first instance, then overturned on appeal and now the case has succeeded in the High Court. We now look forward to pursuing this claim to conclusion."
Enquiries
Litigation Capital Management
Patrick Moloney, Chief Executive Officer
David Collins, Chief Financial Officer
Cavendish (Nomad and Broker)
Tel: 020 7220 0500
Jonny Franklin-Adams and Isaac Hooper (Corporate Finance)
Ella Bedford (Corporate Broking)
www.lcmfinance.com

latest.title: Covenant Waiver Extension
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 8208U
Litigation Capital Management Ltd
02 March 2026
2 March 2026
Litigation Capital Management Limited
("
LCM
" or the "
Company
")
Covenant Waiver Extension
Litigation Capital Management Limited ("LCM"), an alternative asset manager specialising in dispute financing solutions internationally, announces that the debt covenant waiver from Northleaf that was due to expire at the end of February 2026 has been extended to 15 April 2026. During the extended waiver period the interest on the loan remains increased by 2.00% per annum consistent with the initial waiver. There is no additional one-time waiver fee in relation to this extension.
The Strategic Review that was first announced on 15 September 2025 continues to progress and this waiver extension is reflective of Northleaf's ongoing support while LCM works towards a long-term resolution of its capital position. LCM management will provide an update on the progress of the Strategic Review in due course.
Enquiries

latest.title: Increase in Credit Facility and Waiver Extension
latest.content:

2 February 2026
Litigation Capital Management Limited
("
LCM

Company

Increase in Credit Facility and Covenant Waiver Extension
Litigation Capital Management Limited ("LCM"), an alternative asset manager specialising in dispute financing solutions internationally, announces that it has to increase its credit facility with funds managed by Northleaf Capital Partners (Canada) Ltd ("Northleaf") from US$75 million to US$100 million. Furthermore, the debt covenant waiver from Northleaf that was due to expire at the end of January 2026 has been extended to 1 March 2026. During the extended waiver period the interest on the loan remains increased by 2.00% per annum consistent with the initial waiver. There is no additional one-time waiver fee in relation to this extension.
The Strategic Review that was first announced on 15 September 2025 continues to progress and this waiver extension is reflective of Northleaf's ongoing support while LCM works towards a long-term resolution of its capital position. LCM management will provide an update on the progress of the Strategic Review in due course.

latest.title: Positive development on case
latest.content:
Today 14:07
RNS Number : 5649O
Litigation Capital Management Ltd
12 January 2026
12 January 2026
Litigation Capital Management Limited
("
LCM
" or the "
Company
")
Positive development on international arbitration claim
Litigation Capital Management Limited ("LCM"), an alternative asset manager specialising in dispute financing solutions internationally, announces a positive development in the international arbitration claim against the Republic of Poland (Poland) that it funded. As previously announced on 8 October 2024, the funded party achieved a successful outcome, securing two separate awards - one under the Energy Charter Treaty (ECT) of £252 million plus interest; and one under the Australia-Poland Bilateral Investment Treaty (BIT) of £183 million plus interest. There is no double compensation meaning that total damages cannot exceed £252 million plus interest.
Poland commenced set-aside proceedings in Singapore (ECT) and London (BIT) to attempt to annul the original awards. The Singapore proceedings recently concluded and the Singapore court rejected in its entirety Poland's application to set-aside the ECT award. Poland has a right to an appeal and such an appeal must be filed by 6 February 2026. We anticipate an appeal will be filed and expect the Singapore court to resolve such an appeal in the second half of 2026.
LCM has invested into this case alongside Fund I investors with the total investment being US$11.3m with US$2.8 million coming from LCM's own balance sheet and US$8.5 million from Fund I. LCM's returns are calculated as a rising multiple of invested capital over time plus interest. LCM's contractual entitlement on this case currently stands at approximately US$71.9 million with US$18.0 million of that flowing to LCM shareholders and US$53.9 million flowing to Fund I investors. LCM does not anticipate earning a performance fee from Fund I investors on this case as a result of previously announced adverse case results impacting Fund I.
Enquiries
Litigation Capital Management
Patrick Moloney, Chief Executive Officer
David Collins, Chief Financial Officer
Cavendish (Nomad and Broker)
Tel: 020 7220 0500

company_30s.what_they_do: Litigation Capital Management Limited (LCM) funds and manages legal disputes, taking equity or revenue‑share stakes in cases that can generate high returns if the litigation succeeds.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The legal‑finance market is expanding as more companies seek non‑banking capital for lawsuits. LCM’s model allows investors to tap into potentially multi‑million‑pound settlements while giving plaintiffs a boost to pursue claims that would otherwise be unaffordable.
company_30s.current_state: LCM has recently refreshed its case portfolio and extended its credit covenant, indicating a push to keep the engine running. The company is still chasing big wins and its cash position is stretched, but it has not yet posted a fresh profit.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show LCM is tightening its case pipeline and extending a covenant waiver to keep funding lines open. This suggests the firm is still actively looking for high‑value litigation opportunities while managing liquidity.
Earlier announcements highlighted a favorable settlement in a high‑profile case and an update on a strategic review, hinting at a possible shift in how the firm structures deals and raises capital.
The overall pattern is one of survival mode – the company is keeping its doors open, waiting for a big win or a fresh injection of capital to break the current slump.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet desperation – traders are largely silent, but a few optimistic voices hint at a potential rebound if a big case settles.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of users point to the recent settlement win and the covenant extension as signs that LCM is still capable of generating returns. They believe a fresh funding round could unlock upside.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Most observers fear that the legal pipeline is thin and that the company’s cash burn could outpace its case wins. They worry the covenant extension is a temporary bandage, not a long‑term fix.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Initially, the chatter was neutral, but after the settlement announcement a subtle uptick appeared. The mood remains cautious, with traders waiting for a clear signal before committing.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat, meaning no one is currently searching for it. This silence opens the door for a surprise catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: An RNS announcing the outcome of the high‑profile case could deliver a sizable payout, immediately improving cash flow.
A new funding round or a credit facility extension would relieve liquidity pressure and signal confidence to the market.
A court judgment in one of LCM’s cases that expands the scope of a settlement could trigger a price rally.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that breaks the recent support level, a sudden lift in the case‑win news feed, or a press release about new capital. Any of these would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a capitulation cycle – the compression is tight, but the price has not yet found a clear breakout. A breakout above the 52‑week low would be the first sign that the recovery is underway.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The firm sits on a deep drop; if the price climbs to roughly 50 % of its pre‑crash level, that’s a 100 % upside. The recent settlement win and covenant extension suggest a potential liquidity lift. Historically, deep crashes in this sector have bounced dramatically once a case concludes or new capital arrives.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that the litigation pipeline dries out and the company runs out of cash before a settlement materializes. If the covenant expires or a key case fails, the price could fall further into a deeper trough.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: You could lose the remaining 70 % of the price if the company collapses, but a 100 % upside would more than compensate for that risk, giving a 1:1 risk‑reward ratio that is highly attractive for a contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the stock closes above the recent 52‑week low with volume at least 1.5× the average. Place a stop just below the lowest swing low of the last 20 days. Allocate no more than 5 % of your portfolio to this position, scaling in as the trend confirms.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a volume surge or a news release about a case win or new funding. If the price stays flat for 3–4 weeks with low volume, consider exiting or staying out.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy or a court orders a liquidation of its case assets, cut losses immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: LCM’s steep plunge, combined with a quiet market and a possible settlement or capital injection, makes it a prime contrarian long with a clear upside if the price recovers to halfway levels.
